doc_content.summary: The stark-review-deployment-plan skill acts as a Principal Cloud Architect and SRE to perform an adversarial review of infrastructure, migration, and deployment plans. It identifies material flaws prioritized by blast radius rather than focusing on stylistic choices, operating with strict evidence-based criteria across four analytical passes.
key_features: Evaluates plans against 10 critical failure vectors including split-brain scenarios, idempotency, and drift.
Categorizes findings into High, Medium, and Low severities based on execution blocking and data loss risks.
Forces structured output formats including dependency tables, drift tables, and full vector coverage matrices.
Demands concrete failure scenarios and explicit technical fixes for every finding.
Enforces strict evidence rules: passing a vector requires concrete evidence, while unverified external scripts result in a DEFERRED status.
usage_examples: Can you review this deployment plan and poke holes in it? [inline markdown]
Audit my infrastructure cutover document to make sure there are no partial-failure traps.

alt_text: A visualization of the stark-review-deployment-plan skill showing its invocation methods, the 4-pass adversarial review workflow, the 10 specific failure vectors evaluated, and the strict 5-part tabular output structure generated.
